text:
There are two frogs sitting on a dock. They jump into the water.

The first frog sighs dramatically and says how much cooler life would be without those pesky insects buzzing around.

The second frog quips, "I guess you could say I'm trying to evade the stinkbugs."

The first frog glares and replies, "I'm not trying to evade anything!"

They both laugh, roll their eyes, and bounce out of the water to catch insects. A bald eagle swoops down. He catches a fly and sits beside them on the dock.

The frogs stare at the eagle.

"The bald eagle? Wow, you really are majestic."

"Yeah. It's hard to compete with nature's talent."

The frogs nod in agreement.

They listen to cicadas singing their relentless symphony.

The first frog pauses his snacking.

"Well, there is one thing."

The second frog perks up.

"You mean what?"

"To finally catch one of those cicadas and eat it alive."

The frogs
